items.title: Welfare of the people must come first amid oil crisis, says Selangor Sultan
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has expressed concern over rising global oil and gas prices, as well as the shortage of oil supplies resulting from the conflict in the Middle East. His Royal Highness was accompanied by the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin; the Selangor Crown Prince, Tengku Amir Shah; and Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari. #kw: nst, news, Malaysia news, Selangor, sultan, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, Middle East, West Asia, oil, gas, price, crisis, cost of living, wastage © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Sabah weighing redevelopment plans, including land reclamation, after Sandakan fire
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 20 — The state government is looking at several proposal on how or whether to rebuild Kampung Bahagia in Batu Sapi after a major fire which destroyed almost 1,000 houses early yesterday morning. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that several proposals have been made including one for land reclamation to support future housing development. “We have several plans, but I believe it will take one to two weeks before we receive a detailed report on the total number of fire victims and how to proceed.

items.title: Batik Air rolls out up to 35pc fare discounts as travellers seek flexible bookings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Batik Air has introduced discounts of up to 35 per cent on selected economy fares, as airlines respond to growing demand for more flexible travel options. In a statement, the airline said the offer covers its Economy Value and Economy Flexi fares, available for booking until April 30 for travel through January 31, 2027. Both fare types include complimentary date changes, with Economy Value allowing a one-time change and Economy Flexi offering unlimited changes, subject to fare differences.

items.title: Songkran run organiser faces action over water wastage, safety breaches
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) will take action against the organiser of the Songkran Sunset Splash Run for violating event approval conditions. "Any approval granted to the organiser was strictly subject to compliance with the stipulated conditions. "Based on our observation, several aspects of the event implementation did not fully comply with the approved conditions, particularly involving the method of water usage, as well as cleanliness and safety impacts in public areas.

items.title: [UPDATED] UM to act over 'soft porn' theatre performance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya (UM) will take appropriate action over a theatre performance alleged to contain "soft porn" elements during a campus arts competition, the Higher Education Department said today. Higher Education director-general Datuk Dr Azlinda Azman said the ministry had received a report from the university on the matter. She suggested the incident may have stemmed from students being overly enthusiastic in expressing artistic elements, leading them to overlook cultural sensitivities and societal norms.

items.title: Elderly couple killed, four including three soldiers injured in Temerloh crash
items.summary: Perodua Kancil driver Zulkifli Md Yusof, 72, was killed on the spot in the 5.25pm crash, while his wife, Radzuan Mohamad, 67, died several hours later while receiving treatment at the Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital (Hoshas) here. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Nasyim Bahron said the accident involved Zulkifli's Perodua Kancil and a Myvi carrying three soldiers aged between 22 and 27. His wife died at about 8.55pm while receiving treatment at Hoshas," he said in a statement today.

items.title: KLIA baggage system glitch traced to power fault, 1,061 bags shortshipped, says Transport Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A disruption to Kuala Lumpur International Airport’s (KLIA) baggage handling system on April 18 was triggered by a high-voltage power fault at a Bukit Raja substation, the Transport Ministry said today. In a statement, the ministry said preliminary findings showed a 132kV Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) trip at about 4.54pm caused a voltage dip that disrupted operations at Terminal 1, leading to a large-scale system failure affecting baggage handling for both arriving and departing flights. Although the system was supported by uninterruptible power supply (UPS) units, six UPS units were also affected, exposing weaknesses in system resilience, it added.

items.title: 68pct Malaysian elderly have multiple chronic conditions, survey finds
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Diabetes, hypertension and high cholesterol are alarmingly prevalent among Malaysia's elderly, with a new national survey revealing a heavy and growing burden of chronic disease. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said findings from the National Health and Morbidity Survey 2025 (NHMS 2025) showed that 39 per cent of elderly individuals have diabetes, 73 per cent suffer from hypertension and 76 per cent have high cholesterol. He said the survey also found that 68 per cent of the elderly have at least two of these three chronic conditions, while 30 per cent suffer from all three.

items.title: Health Ministry sends medical, psychological support to Sandakan fire victims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry has mobilised aid for victims affected by a massive fire at Kampung Bahagia water village, including deploying medical and psychological support teams to temporary evacuation centres. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said although no fatalities were reported, the ministry is taking proactive steps to ensure the well-being of those affected. He said frontline teams comprising counsellors, nurses, medical assistants and medical officers have been dispatched as part of mobile units to assist victims at the temporary relief centres (PPS).

items.title: Sanusi set to make acting debut in 'The Keris'
items.summary: He said his involvement in the film was limited to a role as a panglima, a warrior commander, who rallies silat practitioners ahead of a bout. He said the character carries elements of Malay heroism inspired by the fighting spirit of historical figures, aimed at stirring motivation among the younger generation. He said he accepted the offer because the role aligned with his interest in elevating Malay arts and culture, particularly silat.

items.title: [UPDATED] Malaysian peacekeepers stay cautious amid situation in Lebanon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's peacekeeping contingent will continue to operate in Lebanon while maintaining a cautious posture due to prevailing safety conditions amid a fragile ceasefire. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt), deployed under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil), remains stable with no major changes in its operational environment. He said Malaysian peacekeepers would continue to carry out their duties based on prevailing security conditions, with operations adjusted accordingly.

items.title: State extends assistance to 2,001 fire victims in Sandakan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 20 — Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor visited fire victims at Kampung Bahagia here and the temporary evacuation centre at Taman Batu Sapi PPR. He also handed over assistance to those affected, including contributions from the State Government, Sabah Islamic Religious Council (Muis), Sabah Baitulmal Corporation and others. He said the assistance was hoped to help ease the victims’ burden, with 2,001 affected individuals, including non-citizens, to receive related support.

items.title: Elderly living alone rises to 18.8pct, threefold increase since 2018
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The National Health and Morbidity Survey 2025 (NHMS 2025) has revealed that 18.8 per cent of elderly individuals in the country are living alone, nearly tripling from 6.3 per cent in 2018. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, however, said 61.3 per cent of this group still report having strong social support despite facing systemic challenges, including limited mobility and financial constraints. In terms of mental health, he said the rate of depression among the elderly has declined to 8.0 per cent from 11.2 per cent in 2018.

items.title: DNeX unit bags services, infrastructure contracts for Saudi Makkah Route initiative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dagang NeXchange Bhd's (DNeX) wholly owned subsidiary Dagang Net Technologies Sdn Bhd has secured site facilitation, logistics and telecommunications infrastructure service contracts to continue supporting Saudi Arabia's Makkah Route initiative for this year's hajj season. In a statement today, DNeX said the contracts cover operations across seven international airports in Malaysia, Indonesia and Türkiye, marking an expansion from the previous year with the addition of a new site in Indonesia. "DNeX has been involved in the Makkah Route programme since 2019.

items.title: Azalina: Corporate manslaughter law could go beyond fines to social penalties
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government is considering financial compensation and social responsibility measures as part of a proposed corporate manslaughter law, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said. Azalina acknowledged concerns that justice may not be served if perpetrators are unable to pay compensation. The tribunal may also make adjustments, as in some countries, compensation is not necessarily limited to monetary payment and could involve social responsibility measures," she said, citing past fatal bus accidents.

items.title: Man charged with possessing 1,451 litres of diesel without licence
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A man was charged in the Sessions Court here today with possessing 1,451 litres of diesel without a licence. Muhammad Zahirafandi Abu Bakar, 30, claimed trial to the charge, which was read to him by the court interpreter before Judge Roslan Hamid. In mitigation, Zahirafandi requested for lower bail.

items.title: Rice reserve stock increased to 300,000 metric tonnes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has increased the country's rice reserve stock to 300,000 metric tonnes as an early measure against potential supply disruptions arising from the global energy crisis. Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the move aims to ensure rice supply remains sufficient and secure, while safeguarding the basic needs of the people. "The government is constantly monitoring the situation and continues to work hard to ensure everyone's basic needs are well taken care of.

items.title: Two brothers killed in rain-triggered three-vehicle crash in Batu Pahat
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A rain-soaked stretch of road turned deadly when a car skidded into oncoming traffic, triggering a three-vehicle pile-up that killed two brothers and left several others injured at Km36.7, Jalan Kluang, Ayer Hitam, yesterday. The deceased brothers, aged 20 and 30, were travelling in a car with two other passengers who are also their siblings. The 42-year-old woman driving the oncoming car sustained minor injuries, while the SUV driver, 35, and his wife also escaped with minor injuries.

items.title: PADU to offer data analytics services to government agencies via portal
items.summary: According to a media statement issued by the ministry on April 20, 2026, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said starting May 2026, the ministry will begin offering PADU data analytics services, known as Analytic-as-a-Service, to government agencies through the PADU analytics portal. The basis of PADU’s development is the integration of data from various agencies with information updated by the public. The inclusiveness and availability of data allow PADU to provide Profile-as-a-Service, Analytics-as-a-Service, Data-as-a-Service, and a Unified Data Platform for government agencies to utilise.

items.title: Man with dwarfism dies after heart complications in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: A 26-year-old man with dwarfism, Mohd Zulhanif Abd Halim, died at 10.15am today at Hospital Tengku Ampuan Afzan (HTAA) after being placed in an induced coma for three days. His father, Abd Halim Sulaiman, 49, said his son died due to heart complications after four days of treatment at HTAA. Abd Halim said he and his wife were able to speak briefly with their son before he was sedated.

items.title: Malaysia's edge in the global circular economy
items.summary: LETTERS: AS the war between the United States and Iran fast approaches the drawn-out peace negotiations stage, the definition of a strategic material is rapidly expanding. What we are witnessing is not merely a geopolitical conflict, but a live stress test of global supply chains, one that in some respects is beginning to rival the disruption experienced during the Covid-19 pandemic. The answer is that materials once considered mundane, like plastics, paper and scrap metals, quickly become critical inputs whose availability determines whether factories run or stall.

items.title: 10,000 Penang consumers spared water cuts during Sungai Prai pipeline works
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: About 10,000 water consumers in Seberang Prai Utara (SPU) and Seberang Prai Tengah (SPT) will not face supply disruptions during final connection works for the third Sungai Prai River-Crossing Pipeline (3rd SP-RCP), scheduled from April 24 to 26. Penang Water Supply Corporation chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the three-day exercise would be carried out using "hot tapping" and "line stopping" technologies at Kampung Bagan Serai in SPU. He said the method allows engineers to connect the new pipeline without shutting down the existing 975mm main pipeline, which channels treated water from the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant to key areas in SPU and SPT.

items.title: Health Ministry: Study shows just 14pc of Malaysians over 60 ‘ageing well’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 20 — Only 14.7 per cent of Malaysians aged 60 and above are living well, the latest National Health and Morbidity Survey showed, a finding the Ministry of Health today described as “a call to action” amid concerns about the country’s care system. “As a minister I am not happy with our progress so far,” Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said at a press conference held after the launch of the report. The Ministry of Health said the findings help inform policy-decisions based on the daily live experiences of older persons, particularly the challenges they face.

items.title: BHEUU should be elevated to full ministry, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said has called for the Legal Affairs Division (BHEUU) to be elevated into a standalone ministry, instead of remaining under the Prime Minister's Department. She said establishing a dedicated ministry would enable the division to operate more effectively and deliver better services to the people. "I hope that next year, the prime minister, if he remains in office, will fulfil his post-election promise to elevate the BHEUU into a full-fledged ministry.

items.title: Local investors comprise RM48.9bil, 58.3pct of Selangor investments in 2025: Sultan Sharafuddin
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah expressed his happiness that more than half of the investment value in Selangor, totalling RM48.9 billion in 2025 or 58.3 per cent, in 2025 was from local investors. The Sultan of Selangor said he was informed that total investment in Selangor last year amounted to RM83.9 billion, exceeding the target of RM65 billion, and that 61,311 job opportunities were created. Meanwhile, the Sultan of Selangor was informed that the state collected RM3.02 billion in state revenue last year, an increase of RM154 million from 2024.

items.title: #NSTviral: Sabah, Penang fans unite in song outside Likas Stadium [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A display of unity among football supporters has gone viral after fans from Sabah and Penang football clubs were seen celebrating together outside theY Likas Stadium here. The 1-minute-23-second video, posted on Facebook by @HarimauMalaya, shows supporters from both sides joyfully jumping, singing and cheering together. Singing and cheering together."

items.title: IJM Land: Enjoy furnishing benefits with a move-in-ready or near-complete home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Homebuyers looking at purchasing a brand-new property can now enjoy various perks thanks to IJM Land's new 'Settled' home ownership campaign. For completed or nearly completed homes, the shorter timeline helps buyers save significantly on bank interest, avoid potential construction delays and gain much-needed financial clarity and certainty. *Availability subject to individual project offerings.

items.title: Naked man found wandering in Bayan Lepas, placed under psychiatric care
items.summary: Deputy northeast district police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the incident occurred about 10.40am on April 16, after police received a public tip-off regarding a man behaving in an unstable manner by the roadside. "Upon arrival, police found the man in the middle of the road naked and behaving erratically. He said the man was subsequently transported by MRCS ambulance to Penang Hospital, accompanied by policemen.

items.title: All 58 MRSM to get full-time wardens next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Starting next year, all 58 Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) will have full-time wardens among former uniformed personnel selected through a strict screening process. Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the selection process will also require candidates to undergo special training to understand Mara's education philosophy. "This follows the effectiveness of a pilot project launched last year at Balik Pulau MRSM and Besut MRSM.

items.title: Sultan of Selangor reprimands state government for lack of seriousness in flood mitigation efforts, despite his past counsel
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 20 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has rebuked the state government for failing to resolve persistent flooding issues despite repeated advisories. According to The Star , the Ruler delivered the reprimand during his royal address at the opening of the Selangor state legislative assembly today. Sultan Sharafuddin noted that he had previously suggested the state seek expertise from overseas, particularly the Netherlands, to find effective solutions for Selangor’s flood problems.

items.title: Selangor Sultan proposes postponement of 2026 Sukma [WATCH]
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has proposed that the state postpone the 22nd Malaysia Games (Sukma) in August until the conflict in the Middle East subsides and the Shah Alam Stadium renovation is fully complete. Present were Tengku Permaisuri Selangor Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, Raja Muda Selangor Tengku Amir Shah, and Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari. Sultan Sharafuddin also decreed that the 2026 Piala Sultan Selangor be cancelled.

items.title: Pahang Sultan, Tengku Ampuan express sympathy over Kampung Bahagia fire
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, and the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, have conveyed their profound sorrow to those affected by the devastating fire at Kampung Bahagia water village in Sandakan, Sabah, yesterday. In a post on the Kesultanan Pahang Facebook page, the palace said Their Royal Highnesses were deeply moved by the tragedy, which caused major destruction, with about 1,000 homes reportedly destroyed and a total of 9,007 residents affected. "The royal couple extend their sympathy to all victims and hope they are granted strength, resilience, and ease in facing these difficult times.

items.title: Malaysia braces for oil price volatility, shifts to biodiesel push
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is preparing for a prolonged period of volatility in global energy markets, with Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir cautioning that oil price stability at previous levels may no longer be realistic amid ongoing tensions and supply chain disruptions. However, the government remains focused on structural adjustments to improve energy resilience, with the gradual expansion of biodiesel usage, moving from B10 blending levels to B15 in phases. "Operationally, increasing biodiesel usage is feasible.

items.title: Ipoh city council assistant town planning officer fined RM3,000 for accepting bribe
items.summary: IPOH: An Ipoh City Council assistant officer was fined RM3,000 by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to accepting a RM2,000 bribe from a business owner two years ago. Judge Meor Sulaiman Ahmad Tarmizi also ordered 53-year-old Syed Nazrudin Syed Mohamed Taib to serve one month in prison if he fails to pay the fine. According to the charge sheet, Syed Nazrudin, an assistant town planning officer attached to the council's Town Planning Department Enforcement Unit, accepted RM2,000 in cash from 49-year-old Chong Yin Mei.

items.title: ‘I got scammed too’: Johari Abdul Ghani exposes fake Miti letter promising RM80,000 investment returns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani today revealed how his name and the ministry were recently exploited in a scam targeting the public. In a Facebook reel today, Johari said he was approached during an open house by an individual who presented him with a letter bearing a Miti letterhead and asked him to verify its authenticity. “In truth, Miti has never issued such a letter.

items.title: MAGA figures finding it tough pushing Trump's campaign to grab Greenland
items.summary: LAST December, Per Berthelsen celebrated a stressful year's end by taking his wife to a restaurant in Nuuk, Greenland's capital. Boassen's behaviour sparked fury in Greenland's governing coalition after Berthelsen mentioned the confrontation on social media. That month, Trump said the US needed to control Greenland.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan’s unique ruler selection system: Who are the Undang Yang Empat and what role are they playing?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A rare constitutional and adat dispute in Negeri Sembilan has thrust the state’s unique traditional system into the national spotlight. What are the Undang? How are Undang selected?

items.title: Malaysia Q1 trade hits record RM789.85bil, led by E&E exports [WATCH]
items.summary: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (MITI) said Malaysia's trade in Q1 grew by 10.4 per cent to RM789.85 billion compared with the same period in 2025. Exports climbed 12.7 per cent to RM426.53 billion, the second-highest quarterly level on record, while imports rose 7.7 per cent to RM363.31 billion. This resulted in a trade surplus of RM63.22 billion.

items.title: Malaysia inflation holds firm at 1.7pct as cost pressures begin to surface: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia's inflation remains broadly contained despite a slight uptick to 1.7 per cent in March 2026, according to the Economy Ministry, signalling that price pressures are still largely under control for now. Presenting the latest data at the Monthly Assembly of the Economy Ministry, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said that inflation rose by 0.3 percentage point from 1.4 per cent in February, indicating a modest increase but relatively within the controlled range. Akmal Nasrullah cautioned that while current inflation levels remain stable, the broader outlook is subject to uncertainty due to the ongoing geopolitical tensions and disruptions in global supply chains.

items.title: China deal helps Malaysia avoid medical device shortages, says Health Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — The supply of medical devices in Malaysia remains stable and under control despite the global energy crisis affecting the availability of certain high-risk imported products. As part of proactive efforts, the Medical Device Authority (MDA) has finalised a strategic agreement with Chinese authorities to increase the supply of resin and naphtha for local industrial use. “This initiative aims to stabilise the supply chain and ensure the continued production of critical medical devices in Malaysia.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s ‘active non-alignment’ helps cut red tape in global defence supply chains amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Malaysia’s pursuit of active non-alignment enables it to maintain working relationships with major powers even during global uncertainties, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Speaking at this year’s Defence Services Asia and National Security Asia conference, Anwar said the policy allows Malaysian suppliers to easily penetrate global supply chains without encountering political complications. Anwar said Malaysian companies manufacture high-quality wing structures and precision components, with one local enterprise holding a Tier 1 supplier status with a leading global aero-engine manufacturer.

items.title: Boat overturns in Kota Belud with eight onboard; six rescued, one dead, search continues for one missing
items.summary: KOTA BELUD, April 20 — A boat carrying eight people capsized off Kampung Tamau Batu Gadung here yesterday afternoon, leaving one dead, one missing, and six survivors. The 18-year-old victim was found by members of the public at about 9.50pm, roughly 700 metres from the capsized boat location, before being taken to hospital by villagers, Malay news outlet BH reported. The 18-year-old was later confirmed dead at Kota Belud Hospital.

items.title: Cross-border cattle run for RM70 each ends with duo in handcuffs in Pasir Mas
items.summary: PASIR MAS, April 20 — A late-night patrol in Kelantan ended with more than just traffic checks after authorities uncovered a lorry packed with 13 cows being moved without proper paperwork. Two men aged 22 and 25 were arrested on the spot in Kampung Manan after General Operations Force (GOF) officers stopped the vehicle during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 9.30pm yesterday, New Straits Times reported. He said the lorry and cattle were seized immediately.

items.title: Ex-Undang Luak Sungai Ujong’s move to depose Negeri Sembilan Ruler ‘disloyal’, should face legal action, says official
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A former Undang Luak Sungai Ujong’s move to sign and read a declaration purporting to depose the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, has been condemned as an act of disloyalty and without any authority. Telaga Undang official Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri said Datuk Mubarak Dohak’s declaration was carried out without any customary or constitutional mandate, Malay news outlet BH reported today. “The Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu also never gave him a mandate to do so.

items.title: Sarawak eyes hydrogen as diesel prices remain volatile
items.summary: KUCHING, April 20 — Sarawak is strengthening its push towards alternative energy sources, particularly hydrogen, in response to ongoing volatility in global diesel prices linked to geopolitical tensions and market dynamics. According to Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg, hydrogen production is expected to become relatively more cost-competitive than diesel, supported by advances in electrolysis technology, including improvements in membrane efficiency. “With today’s diesel prices, the production of hydrogen will be cheaper than diesel; the electrolysis process will be cheaper because of the new membrane in the technology.

items.title: Reduced ferries spark outcry as Langkawi groups demand govt intervention
items.summary: LANGKAWI, April 20 — The government has been urged to step in and resolve the issue of reduced ferry trips to Langkawi, which stakeholders claim is hurting tourism and disrupting the daily lives of residents. Anak Muda Madani Kedah chairman Datuk Zuraidi Rahim said the reduction of ferry trips, from five to three daily services since March 25, has led to a decline in tourist arrivals to the island. “About 70 per cent of tourists rely on ferry services to reach Langkawi.

items.title: ‘We thought they were fireworks’: Kota Tinggi traders recount chaos before three killed in shooting
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 20 — Panic gripped a business area in Taman Kota Jaya here yesterday after witnesses reported hearing a loud explosion that was initially thought to be firecrackers, but was later found to be a shooting that left three people dead. He said the suspect then went to his vehicle before returning to the scene. Everyone was panicked, so we immediately closed shop,” he said.

items.title: No authority, no validity: Negeri Sembilan MB rejects bid to unseat state Ruler
items.summary: He added that Mubarak’s dismissal as the incumbent of Datuk Klana Petra Luak Sungei Ujong was effected on May 13, 2025, in accordance with the customs and traditions of the Telaga Undang, Waris Klana Hulu, Sungei Ujong. Aminuddin also said that the Special Sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) Negeri Sembilan held on April 17 agreed to advise and accept the consensus of the Telaga Undang, Waris Klana Hulu, Luak Sungei Ujong, which dismissed Mubarak as the 10th Datuk Klana Petra Luak Sungei Ujong. He added that this is in accordance with Clause 14(3) of the state constitution, based on 33 offences related to the customs and traditions of the Telaga Undang, Waris Klana Hulu, Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Perlis assembly delay not political, will sit before June 8, says MB
items.summary: KANGAR, April 20 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah has assured that the state legislative assembly will sit before June 8, with the new date to be announced next week. He said the postponement of the sitting was not related to any issue of a vote of no confidence or uncertainty over support for the state government under his leadership. The Perlis state assembly was originally scheduled to sit from April 21 to 23.

items.title: Economists back govt’s call for prudent spending as Malaysia faces supply‑driven inflation
items.summary: Lee Heng Guie, executive director of the Socio-Economic Research Centre (SERC), said the current situation is driven by supply constraints, warning that stimulating demand at this stage could worsen inflation. “You have a supply shock. You don’t want to add fire to demand when you have a supply shock,” he said.

items.title: Police arrest 71-year-old, seize shotgun after three shot dead in Kota Tinggi; debt motive probed
items.summary: In a statement on Facebook, Johor police chief CP Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that the victims — two local men and a foreign woman aged between 37 and 63 — were believed to have been shot with a shotgun and were pronounced dead at the scene. He said police received a report of the shooting at about 1.30pm before arresting the suspect roughly 200 metres from the scene at around 2pm. He said police will apply for a remand order at the Kota Tinggi Magistrates’ Court tomorrow.

items.title: DPM Zahid: Central disaster committee to coordinate aid for 9,000 displaced in Kampung Bahagia fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — The Central Disaster Management Committee will coordinate efforts with the federal and Sabah governments in terms of basic needs, temporary accommodation and welfare of fire victims in Kampung Bahagia, Batu Sapi, Sandakan. “I am saddened to see the massive fire incident involving hundreds of houses in Kampung Bahagia, Batu Sapi, Sandakan. Some residents placed household appliances and items that were successfully rescued from a fire in an open area in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan April 19, 2026.

items.title: Eight injured in lorry-tour van collision near Menara TH Selborn on Jalan Tun Razak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Eight people were injured in an accident involving a lorry and a tour van near the National Library of Malaysia on Jalan Tun Razak here today. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the victims included a 56-year-old lorry driver who sustained eye injuries and a 46-year-old lorry attendant who suffered leg injuries. The statement said a team from the Titiwangsa Fire and Rescue Station, together with a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) arrived at the scene at 4.14 pm after receiving an emergency call at 4.04 pm.

items.title: At DAP’s 60th anniversary, Nga calls on members to remain humble and service‑focused
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, April 19 — DAP deputy national chairman Nga Kor Ming today called on all party members to remain steadfast in their commitment to the people by staying “humble, hungry and focused in delivering service to the people” as the party marks its 60th anniversary. Highlighting the party’s transition from advocacy to governance, Nga who is also the Minister of Housing and Local Government shared the ministry’s key achievements under his leadership. Nga further highlighted the successful implementation of 15,083 infrastructure projects across the country, reflecting the Madani’s government’s commitment to improving basic amenities at all local councils level.

items.title: Cops launch manhunt in Alor Gajah after cable‑theft suspects flee inspection, leave tools in abandoned car
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, April 19 — Police are hunting for three suspects believed to be involved in cable theft after they fled while being stopped for inspection at the Kelemak traffic lights here early this morning. Alor Gajah district police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said in the 2.30 am incident, the three suspects, who were travelling in a white Volkswagen Polo, were spotted behaving suspiciously before a police Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) unit instructed the driver to stop for inspection. However, he said the driver sped off, prompting the MPV personnel to pursue the suspects to Kampung Pegoh.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Sarawak’s renewable energy, digital push sets foundation for future economic growth
items.summary: KUCHING, April 19 — The emphasis by Sarawak’s leadership on renewable energy-based economic development, the digital economy and education shows that the state is on the right track to becoming a leader in the future economy, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said Sarawak is charting a progressive future direction through economic development based on gas, renewable energy and downstream industries, thereby creating job opportunities and improving the well-being of the people. “This reflects the Premier’s focus on safeguarding rights, long-term prosperity and the continuity of Sarawak’s development,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Zambry: Govt assures open access to higher education for SPM, STPM leavers
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 19 — There is no fixed limit on the number of students admitted into higher education institutions for Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) and Sijil Tinggi Pelajaran Malaysia (STPM) holders; instead, they are given every possible opportunity to further their studies based on their interests and qualifications. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said around 100,000 SPM leavers and 75,000 STPM leavers this year have the opportunity to continue their studies, not only at universities but also in skills-based fields such as Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). Also present were Sabah Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin and UMS Vice-Chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Kasim Mansor.

items.title: Ampang hit by two landslides after heavy downpour, no casualties recorded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — The Selangor Fire and Rescue Department received reports of two landslides along Jalan Indah 4, Kampung Ampang Indah, Ampang, following heavy rain this afternoon, with no casualties reported. Assistant director of the department’s Operations Division, Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar, said the first incident involved a hillside collapse behind a house at about 3.35 pm. Both incidents did not involve any structural damage or casualties, he said, adding that five personnel from the Ampang Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Selangor police chief: Sungai Buloh Prison escapee recaptured after nine days on the run
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — A prisoner who slipped custody earlier this month has been recaptured after nine days on the run, Selangor police said. State police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed the detainee was apprehended at about 5pm yesterday along Jalan Batu 14, Jalan Gombak Lama. “The assistance provided was crucial in ensuring the prisoner was successfully recaptured to maintain public safety and order,” he said.

items.title: Wet weather continues into the evening for Klang Valley and rest of peninsula, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit the federal capital and 10 states until 7pm tonight. MetMalaysia in a statement announced that the states affected are Kedah involving Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu; Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim). Similar weather is expected to hit Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat); Pahang (Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin); Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman) and Kuala Lumpur.
